summary: Matched a small set of Yoshi special-move functions in ftYs_SpecialN.c, including one helper that writes a Vec3 from Yoshi ext_attr fields and two physics callbacks that forward directly to ft_80084F3C. The PR also refined ftSeakAttributes field types from s32 to f32 at offsets x38, x40, x44, and x48, with comments tying those type choices to matching remaining Sheik SpecialHi functions. Evidence indicates the Seak header edits were required context for matching, but no review comments explain further rationale.
what_changed: Implemented ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD88(Vec3* arg0), reading Yoshi ext_attr from gFtDataList[FTKIND_YOSHI] and writing arg0->x from fp->x34_scale.z, arg0->y from fp->x40, and arg0->z as 0.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialHi_Phys(ftYs_GObj* gobj) as a direct call to ft_80084F3C(gobj).
Implemented ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Phys(ftYs_GObj* gobj) as a direct call to ft_80084F3C(gobj).
Changed ftSeakAttributes fields x38, x40, x44, and x48 from s32 to f32.
Preserved and extended inline header comments documenting why ftSeakAttributes offsets are typed as floats, referencing matching context from Sheik SpecialHi work.
smart_moves: Used the fighter data list and FTKIND_YOSHI to access Yoshi-specific ext_attr rather than hard-coding unrelated data paths.
Recognized tiny physics callbacks as wrapper functions around the shared helper ft_80084F3C, avoiding overcomplication.
Adjusted struct field types in ftSeakAttributes based on surrounding matching evidence, which can unblock multiple functions that depend on the same attribute layout.
Kept explanatory comments in the header next to the uncertain/derived field types, making the matching rationale searchable for later work.
decomp_lessons: For fighter attribute structs, field signedness/integer-vs-float choices can be determined by how multiple functions load and use the same offsets; fixing the header can be necessary before unrelated-looking functions match.
Very small action callbacks may legitimately compile to a single call into a shared physics helper; do not assume every callback needs unique logic.
When reading ext_attr through gFtDataList, the fighter kind enum can matter for matching and clarity.
Comments that name the function or scratch context that justified a struct field type are useful because later decomp work may otherwise revert or second-guess the type.
naming_conventions: Yoshi special functions use the ftYs_ prefix, for example ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD88, ftYs_SpecialHi_Phys, and ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Phys.
Sheik-related attribute struct is named ftSeakAttributes in src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/types.h.
Fighter kind constants such as FTKIND_YOSHI are used when indexing gFtDataList.
Unidentified attribute offsets remain named by offset, such as x38, x40, x44, x48, and x4C, until better semantic names are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed ftSeakAttributes fields at offsets x38, x40, x44, and x48 from s32 to f32 to align codegen with observed float usage.
Matched ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD88 by writing three Vec3 components explicitly, including a zero z component.
Matched ftYs_SpecialHi_Phys and ftYs_SpecialLwLanding_Phys as direct tail/simple wrappers to ft_80084F3C.
Used existing comments about decomp.me scratch work and other Sheik functions as evidence for struct layout/type corrections.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states that the ftSeak types.h changes were related to matching the remaining functions, but does not provide detailed reviewer discussion.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftSeakAttributes x38/x40/x44/x48 or confirm their semantics.
Search for other ftYs special callbacks that are simple wrappers around ft_80084F3C.
Check subsequent Yoshi SpecialN PRs for semantic names for ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD88 and the Vec3 it initializes.
Investigate Sheik SpecialHi matching work that referenced ftSeakAttributes float fields.
